_comment: Demo for #817: before the fix, a whole second-rank of soldiers that arrived at melee range still carrying full march speed got driven into a hard backward recoil (peaking near KNOCKBACK_SPEED_MAX) by SoldierEnemyContact's continuous contact impulse, stranding that rank ~50-75 world units behind the line for several hundred ticks with nothing but ordinary jog-paced arrival steering to bring it back. Authored fresh (not a copy of demos/inputs/all-out-attack-rank-check.json): a different seed, different spawn coordinates, and a slightly bigger 90-soldier line further right on the field. Selects the player unit (uid 0) and arms both the soldier-ID overlay and the engaged-soldier highlight (Settings.show_engaged_highlight) so the front ranks staying tightly packed through the clash -- instead of a chunk of the highlight visibly detaching and drifting backward -- is the tell. Camera holds a wide establishing frame, then eases into a tight zoom on the clash point once the lines are about to meet, then holds through the whole prolonged press so the recovered/steady front is visible for several seconds -- one hold, one eased move, one long hold, per the no-jerky-camera convention. The `steps` timing (an early select, then arm All-out attack shortly after) is a generic setup gesture, not scenario-specific, so it isn't independently re-derived here. The `state` list IS scenario-specific and was derived from a dense (every-10-tick, then every-1-tick around the clash) dump-state.sh sweep of THIS scenario: clash onset lands at tick 126 (engaged flips false->true between 125 and 126), the formation compresses to its tightest (spread ~23-24 world units) around ticks 190-200, then gradually re-widens as casualties reduce the file count through the rest of the grind -- the listed ticks below sample the pre-clash march, the exact clash tick, the post-clash compaction, and several points through the later grind, none copied from all-out-attack-rank-check.json's own list.
